name: Tradicional
description: Baseline forfeits — flirtatious, not explicit. The core Picante experience.

forfeits.text: {{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} a truth (no yes/no questions). Answer honestly → {{biasedRandom}} takes 2. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 2.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} a truth (no yes/no questions). Answer honestly → {{biasedRandom}} takes 3. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 3.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} a truth (no yes/no questions). Answer honestly → {{biasedRandom}} takes 4. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 4.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} a truth (no yes/no questions). Answer honestly → {{biasedRandom}} takes 5. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 5.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} a truth (no yes/no questions). Answer honestly → {{biasedRandom}} takes 6. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 6.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} a truth (no yes/no questions). Answer honestly → {{biasedRandom}} takes 7. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 7.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} a truth (no yes/no questions). Answer honestly → {{biasedRandom}} takes 8. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 8.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} a truth (no yes/no questions). Answer honestly → {{biasedRandom}} takes 9. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 9.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} a truth (no yes/no questions). Answer honestly → {{biasedRandom}} takes 10. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 10.
True or false? {{drawer}} makes a statement about themselves. Distribute 10 among the wrong-guessers. All guesses correct → {{drawer}} takes 10. {{drawer}} refuses to play → {{drawer}} takes 10.
{{drawer}} reveals a spicy secret about themselves. News to everyone → {{drawer}} distributes 10. Anyone already knew → {{drawer}} takes 10. {{drawer}} refuses to reveal → {{drawer}} takes 10.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} something darker — deeper, more intimate, more revealing. Answer → {{biasedRandom}} takes 15. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 15.
Ace of Secretos. The three players with the highest penalty counts catch a blast: 15 / 10 / 5. {{drawer}} takes an extra 5 on top.
{{biasedRandom}} gives {{drawer}} a dare. Complete it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 2. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 2.
{{biasedRandom}} gives {{drawer}} a dare. Complete it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 3. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 3.
{{biasedRandom}} gives {{drawer}} a dare. Complete it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 4. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 4.
{{biasedRandom}} gives {{drawer}} a dare. Complete it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 5. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 5.
{{biasedRandom}} gives {{drawer}} a dare. Complete it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 6. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 6.
{{biasedRandom}} gives {{drawer}} a dare. Complete it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 7. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 7.
{{biasedRandom}} gives {{drawer}} a dare. Complete it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 8. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 8.
{{biasedRandom}} gives {{drawer}} a dare. Complete it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 9. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 9.
{{biasedRandom}} gives {{drawer}} a dare. Complete it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 10. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 10.
{{drawer}} dares {{biasedRandom}} to do something. Complete it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 10. Refuse → {{drawer}} takes 10.
{{drawer}} is invited to send a risqué message to {{biasedRandom}}. Send it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 10. Refuse → {{drawer}} takes 10.
{{biasedRandom}} gives {{drawer}} a significantly harder dare. Complete it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 15. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 15.
Ace of Riesgos. The player with the lowest penalty count takes half the gap to the highest. {{drawer}} takes an extra 5.

'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} finishes the line everyone votes on — e.g. most likely to…, least likely to…, biggest…. Group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 2.

'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} finishes the line everyone votes on — e.g. most likely to…, least likely to…, biggest…. Group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 3.

'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} finishes the line everyone votes on — e.g. most likely to…, least likely to…, biggest…. Group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 4.

'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} finishes the line everyone votes on — e.g. most likely to…, least likely to…, biggest…. Group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 5.

'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} finishes the line everyone votes on — e.g. most likely to…, least likely to…, biggest…. Group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 6.

'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} finishes the line everyone votes on — e.g. most likely to…, least likely to…, biggest…. Group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 7.

'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} finishes the line everyone votes on — e.g. most likely to…, least likely to…, biggest…. Group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 8.

'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} finishes the line everyone votes on — e.g. most likely to…, least likely to…, biggest…. Group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 9.

'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} finishes the line everyone votes on — e.g. most likely to…, least likely to…, biggest…. Group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 10.
{{drawer}} names a shared dare for themselves and {{biasedRandom}}. Both complete it → distribute 10 among the rest of the group. Either refuses → refuser takes 10. Both refuse → 5 each.
{{biasedRandom}} is invited to share a kiss with {{drawer}}. Both in → distribute 10 among everyone else. One declines → they take 10. Both decline → 5 each.

{{drawer}} names a significantly harder shared dare for themselves and {{biasedRandom}}. Both complete it → distribute 15 among the rest of the group. Either refuses → refuser takes 15. Both refuse → {{drawer}} takes 8, {{biasedRandom}} takes 7.
Ace of Sorbo. The three players with the lowest penalty counts catch up: 15 / 10 / 5. {{drawer}} takes an extra 5.
Mini-game: Nose. {{drawer}} calls 'NOSE'. Last to touch their index finger to their nose loses 2. Refuse to play → take 2.
Mini-game: Three-word. Starting with {{drawer}}, build a sentence one word at a time around the table. First to break the chain (or stall too long) loses 3. Refuse to play → take 3.
Mini-game: Categories. {{drawer}} picks a category, then names an item. Going around, each player names another. Repeat or blank → lose 4. Refuse to play → take 4.
Mini-game: Thumb War. {{drawer}} picks any challenger. Loser takes 5. Refuse to play → take 5.
Mini-game: Countdown. {{drawer}} counts silently in their head. Everyone shouts 'STOP' when they think 10 seconds have passed. Furthest from 10 loses 6. Refuse to play → take 6.
Mini-game: Rhyme Chain. {{drawer}} says a word. Going around, each player must rhyme. Break the chain → lose 7. Refuse to play → take 7.
Mini-game: Staring. {{drawer}} picks any challenger. First to blink, laugh, or look away loses 8. Refuse to play → take 8.

Mini-game: Odds. {{drawer}} challenges any player: 'what are the odds you'd X?' They pick a ceiling. On 3-2-1 both say a number in range. Match → challenged does it or cops out (takes 9). No match → {{drawer}} takes 9.
Mini-game: Most-Likely. {{drawer}} poses a 'who's most likely to X?' question. Group votes. Most-voted takes 10. Refuse to play → {{drawer}} takes 10.
Drawer's Challenge. {{drawer}} invents a short challenge (physical, trivia, or dexterity) and picks any player to attempt it. Complete → {{drawer}} takes 10. Fail or refuse → target takes 10.
1v1 Rock-Paper-Scissors. {{drawer}} picks any player. One round. Loser takes 10. Either refuses → refuser takes 10.
Picante Roulette. On '3', every player (including {{drawer}}) simultaneously points at one other. Most-pointed-at takes 15. Tie → {{drawer}} chooses. Anyone refuses to point → they take 15.
Ace of Locura. The player with the highest penalty count takes half the gap to the lowest. {{drawer}} takes an extra 5.
